Mt. Battle (Battle Mode - Singles)

This page is about the version of Mt. Battle that is accessed through Battle Mode. For information about the version accessed during Story Mode, please see this page for the Mt. Battle location and this page for the Trainers in the 100-battle challenge.

In addition to the Story Mode version of Mt. Battle, there is also a Battle Mode version with stronger Trainers that the player can choose to take on. Unlike the version in Story Mode, enemy Trainers' Pokémon will scale according to the player's Pokémon's levels. They will match the level of the player's highest-leveled Pokémon, but they will not go below level 50.

Ho-Oh can be obtained by defeating this version of Mt. Battle. However, it can only be obtained by first purifying all 48 Shadow Pokémon in Story Mode, and using a team of Pokémon that the player has currently in Story Mode. Ho-Oh is level 70 and will be added to the first empty spot in the player's PC.

Defeating each Area rewards the player with Poké Coupons, which can be exchanged for items in the lobby of Mt. Battle in Story Mode or in the Poké Coupon exchange in the menu of Battle Mode. Each Area has a set amount of Poké Coupons, but each Legendary Pokémon the player uses will drop the reward by approximately 13%. The rewards for each area are given at the beginning of each area's data.

  • Area 1 (1-10)
  • Area 2 (11-20)
  • Area 3 (21-30)
  • Area 4 (31-40)
  • Area 5 (41-50)
  • Area 6 (51-60)
  • Area 7 (61-70)
  • Area 8 (71-80)
  • Area 9 (81-90)
  • Area 10 (91-100)

Area 1 (Battles 1-10)

The reward is 50 Poké Coupons.
